summary.brief: Heffelfinger, M. v. Shen, L.. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Appellee is a 68 year-old woman who resides in Chester County, Pennsylvania.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Shen is licensed to practice dentistry in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania and is the sole owner of her professional corporation, Shen Smiles, PC.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appellee went to Dr. Shen for dental care with a lesion present on the left side of her tongue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Throughout more than six visits in 2018, each initiated by Appellee, the lesion continued to grow and worsen.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Shen prescribed palliative treatment but failed to refer Appellee for specialist care, perform an oral cancer screening or biopsy for diagnosis of the lesion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appellee visited an oral surgeon, Niral Parikh, DDS, on December 28, 2018, who immediately recognized the lesion as possible squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appellee underwent treatment for squamous cell carcinoma from January thru June of 2019.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The resulting extensive, invasive treatment caused permanent injury including speech deficits, lymphedema, scarring, and a continued risk of recurrence.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Expert testimony presented at trial showed that the care provided by Dr. Shen fell well below the standard of care that requires referring an oral lesion within two weeks of observation without improvement.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Dr. Koch testified extensively about what could have been, and what actually resulted, regarding the treatment of [Appellee].
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The jury found Dr. Shen negligent and awarded Appellee $3 million in compensatory damages; the jury also found Dr. Shen’s reckless conduct warranted $8 million in punitive damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Jury finding

evidence_index.text: Judgment was subsequently entered in favor of Appellee, and jointly against Appellants, in the total amount of $11,459,513.69.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury’s verdict bears a reasonable resemblance to the proven damages, and we are precluded from reweighing the evidence and substituting our judgment for that of the factfinder.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.chief_complaint: Lesion on the left side of the tongue
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports a lesion on the left side of her tongue that has been present and worsening over multiple visits in 2018.

focused_exam.oral: Lesion present on the left side of the tongue

requestables.name: Oral Surgeon Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with an oral surgeon for evaluation of the tongue lesion

reveal.result_summary: Lesion recognized as possible squamous cell carcinoma

requestables.name: Biopsy of Tongue Lesion
requestables.description: Biopsy to confirm the nature of the tongue lesion

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a tongue lesion

key_events.event: First visit to Dr. Shen with tongue lesion
Lesion continues to grow over six visits

timeline_phases.name: Critical Decision Point
timeline_phases.description: Decision not to refer for biopsy

timeline_phases.name: Diagnosis and Treatment
timeline_phases.description: Diagnosis and treatment of squamous cell carcinoma

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with a persistent oral lesion
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing this oral lesion?

options.description: Refer to an oral surgeon for biopsy
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care for persistent oral lesions
Inadequate for a potentially malignant lesion

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not refer for specialist care

expected_action_court.description: Referral to an oral surgeon should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony established referral as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to refer for biopsy led to delayed diagnosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral and biopsy should have been performed

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed permanent injuries including speech deficits and lymphedema
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Speech deficits, lymphedema, scarring, risk of recurrence

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to refer for biopsy
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have prevented extensive treatment

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Referral would have led to early diagnosis and less invasive treatment

court_findings.finding: Dr. Shen breached duty of care
